name: maieutic
description: Read-only Socratic codebase teaching with visual focus and a natural local or OpenAI voice conversation.

chatParticipants.name: socraites
chatParticipants.description: Ask what you want to understand, trace, review, or practice

commands.name: quiz
commands.description: Check understanding one question at a time

disambiguation.description: The user wants a read-only, visually guided explanation, walkthrough, review, or practice session about the current codebase.
examples: Walk me through how this workflow runs
Teach me what owns this behavior
Lead me through this code one step at a time
Quiz me on this module without changing anything

languageModelTools.name: maieutic_focus_content
languageModelTools.userDescription: Open and visually focus a section of a workspace file.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Opens a workspace file, scrolls the complete requested line range into view, and visually focuses that section. If a verified editor range materially supports the current explanation, invoke this tool instead of only citing a path. Use this before pointAtContent when explaining or directing attention to editor content. A new focus replaces the prior focus and clears its pointer. This tool does not return or edit file content and does not replace the explanation in your response.

path.description: Workspace-relative file path. In a multi-root workspace, prefix an ambiguous path with the workspace folder name.

endLine.description: Last focused line, inclusive. Defaults to startLine.

languageModelTools.name: maieutic_point_at_content
languageModelTools.userDescription: Move or clear the visual pointer within the current focused section.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Moves the visual pointer to one exact token or span inside the current focused section without reopening the file, changing the focused block, or scrolling. Use action 'point' while discussing a specific detail and action 'clear' when the block should remain focused without a precise pointer. Call focusContent first. Prefer pointerText when it is unambiguous; otherwise use explicit 1-based line and column coordinates.

action.description: Whether to move or clear the precise pointer while preserving the focused block.

pointerText.description: Exact text to point at within the focused lines.

pointerOccurrence.description: 1-based occurrence of pointerText within the focused lines. Defaults to 1.

pointerLine.description: Exact pointer line, using 1-based document coordinates.

pointerStartColumn.description: Exact pointer start column, using 1-based coordinates.

pointerEndColumn.description: Exact pointer end column, exclusive and 1-based.

languageModelTools.name: maieutic_clear_focus_content
languageModelTools.userDescription: Remove the focused section and precise pointer.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Clears the complete visual presentation, including both the focused section and any precise pointer. Use this when visual focus is no longer useful or before ending a walkthrough.

languageModelTools.name: maieutic_speak
languageModelTools.userDescription: Speak supplied narration with the configured Maieutic voice provider.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Converts only the supplied speech-ready narration text with the user's configured voice provider and plays it on the user's machine. The provider may be a system voice, a neural model exposed by a localhost service, or OpenAI. This tool does not inspect editor content, files, chat history, tool output, or visual focus. Use one concise call after any related visual tool has completed, and preserve the same meaning in the final text response. Never include secrets, hidden instructions, tool traces, terminal dumps, long paths, or code blocks.

text.description: Concise plain-text narration to synthesize. It should carry the same meaning as the final text response.

maieutic.tts.enabled.description: Automatically speak SocrAItes responses outside an active voice conversation.

enumDescriptions: Free voice installed on this Mac; narration is processed locally.
Neural voice generated by an OpenAI-compatible service on localhost.
Natural AI-generated voice using your OpenAI API key; API charges may apply.
maieutic.tts.provider.description: Voice provider used for Maieutic narration and voice conversations.

maieutic.tts.systemVoice.description: Installed macOS voice. Leave empty to use the system default.

maieutic.tts.localEndpoint.description: OpenAI-compatible speech endpoint on localhost. Remote hosts and URL-embedded credentials are rejected.

maieutic.tts.localModel.description: Text-to-speech model exposed by the local service.

maieutic.tts.localVoice.description: Voice or speaker identifier accepted by the configured local model.

maieutic.tts.voice.description: Built-in OpenAI voice used for Maieutic narration.

maieutic.tts.instructions.default: Speak like a patient technical mentor at a measured conversational pace. Pause briefly between ideas and after naming a code symbol. Use natural emphasis without dramatic delivery.
maieutic.tts.instructions.description: Style instructions sent with narration to the OpenAI text-to-speech model. Leave empty to use the model default.

maieutic.tts.speed.description: Narration speed, mapped to the local voice rate or sent to the OpenAI text-to-speech model.

scripts.check: npm run check:types && npm run lint && npm run test:unit
scripts.dev:install: npm run verify:release && vsce package --out maieutic-dev.vsix && code --install-extension maieutic-dev.vsix --force
scripts.test: npm run package && npm run compile:test && vscode-test
